parsed.title: The First Principles of Language: Information Theory and Markov Chains
parsed.scope: This section establishes the mathematical lens through which language was first quantified. It will cover Claude Shannon’s Information Theory, defining entropy as a measure of linguistic predictability, and transition into how these concepts led to the first generative models: Markov Chains and n-grams.
